description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet and a front panel featuring a ribbed, golden-brown speaker grille. The lower section of the front panel displays a horizontal frequency indicator with a glass or plastic cover, flanked by several rotary dials and switches for tuning and volume control. The left side of the front panel has a red badge with the 'Grundig' brand name, and the right side is labeled 'Rossini 4R' along with other model information. The sides and back of the radio are made of dark wood, with no visible openings or controls. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-20th-century home radios, and the object appears to be well-preserved with clear, legible markings. The radio's construction suggests a combination of wood, plastic, and metal components, with a likely hollow interior housing electronic parts.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a golden-brown ribbed speaker grille, rotary dials, and a horizontal frequency indicator on the front. The object features the 'Grundig' brand and 'Rossini 4R' model markings.
description_view_0: The front view shows the ribbed speaker grille, frequency indicator, rotary dials, and brand/model markings.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain, dark wooden panel with no visible features.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a flat, featureless dark wooden back panel.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing a plain, dark wooden surface.
